condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I decomposed the question — "Will Donald Trump **fail to attend** LIV Golf New York 2026?" — into two channels, since YES resolves if **either** the event is cancelled **or** Trump is absent. I ran two nested research streams: (1) the status and financial health of the Bedminster event, and (2) Trump's history of attending LIV events at his own clubs.

## Key Evidence

**Event status (favors NO):**
- LIV Golf New York at `Trump National Bedminster` is confirmed for **August 6–9, 2026**, announced Jan 26, 2026, tickets on sale, and **still live on LIV's official schedule** as of July 12, 2026 — not cancelled or postponed.
- It is widely characterized as the **most secure** of LIV's four remaining events, explicitly because of the Trump–Saudi relationship.

**Financial crisis (favors YES via cancellation):**
- Per the Financial Times, Saudi PIF delivered only **~$200M of ~$600M** committed for 2026 (~$66M in May, ~$130M in June), leaving ~$400M outstanding.
- PIF is **withdrawing funding after the 2026 season**; LIV runs on monthly injections that insiders fear could dry up before the season ends.
- The **New Orleans event was already postponed** (April 2026); CEO Scott O'Neil **refused to guarantee** any of the final four events; LIV filed a **WARN Act** layoff notice in July 2026. The Michigan Team Championship is seen as most at risk ("I don't think they'll ever get to Michigan").

**Trump attendance (strongly favors NO):**
- Trump has a **consistent multi-year record**: played the 2022 Bedminster pro-am, hosted/attended 2023, attended Doral 2022–2025, and — as a sitting president — attended **LIV Virginia on May 9, 2026** from an 18th-hole suite.
- Bedminster is his own club, early August is peak season for him there, and resolution criteria are lenient (he only needs to be **on the grounds** during the four-day window). No source explicitly confirms a 2026 Bedminster plan.

## Conclusion

I built a two-component estimate: **P(event runs) ≈ 82%** (safest remaining event, but genuine funding crisis) × **P(Trump present | event runs) ≈ 92%** (documented record, own club, lenient criteria) ≈ **P(NO) ≈ 0.75**, so **P(YES) ≈ 0.25**. My reflection factors initially had inconsistent signs (implying ~0.965 for the dominant factor); I caught and corrected this so positive logits = YES = fail-to-attend, yielding a factor-implied ~0.195, which I nudged **up to 0.25** to reflect compounding cancellation + absence tail risk. I emphasized that the **dominant driver of YES is event cancellation, not Trump's willingness**. A premortem review approved the forecast, independently estimating P(YES) ≈ 0.22–0.27. Final: **P(YES) = 0.25**.

capability_gaps: No prediction-market lookup was performed; a Polymarket/Kalshi query tool (or its use) would have provided a market anchor for the cancellation component.
subtle_bugs: AskNews tools consistently 403'd on Metaculus-tier restrictions (model choice, n_articles>10, credit overage), silently wasting several turns before the agent fell back to web_search — a recurring friction pattern rather than a one-off.

classification.approach: Two-component decomposition — P(event runs) × P(Trump present | runs) — grounded in news research on LIV's finances and Trump's attendance record.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether LIV can financially deliver the Bedminster event amid an acute PIF funding crisis; Trump's personal attendance is near-certain if the event runs.

risk_flags.evidence: The agent did not check any prediction markets (Polymarket/Kalshi/Manifold) or betting odds for a topical, market-tradeable question about a specific dated event. This is a standard reference point that was skipped.

The event-cancellation estimate (~18%) leans heavily on Bedminster being the 'safest' event, but multiple authoritative sources (ESPN, CBS, FT, FOS) describe an acute crisis where 'every remaining tournament is on the fence' and the CEO refused to guarantee ANY event. A somewhat higher cancellation probability (and thus higher P(YES)) is defensible; the agent's nudge from 0.195 to 0.25 partly addresses this but the point estimate could still understate systemic collapse risk.

strengths: Correctly identified the disjunctive resolution structure (YES if event cancelled OR Trump absent) and decomposed into P(event runs) × P(present | runs) rather than treating it as a single attendance question.

Detected and corrected a sign-inconsistency in its own reflection factors (initial dominant factor implied ~0.965 with wrong signs), re-running with positive=YES convention to reach a coherent ~0.195 factor-implied value.
Correctly disambiguated a dating trap: confirmed Trump's LIV Virginia attendance was May 2026 (not 2025) and that Bedminster had no 2025 event, avoiding a spurious 'annual attendance' base rate.
Identified the leading indicator (UK event July 23-26 as a signal for the August Bedminster event) and the correct order-of-risk among remaining events.
Transparently attributed the dominant YES driver to event cancellation risk rather than Trump's willingness, which matches the evidence.
weaknesses: Did not consult prediction markets or sportsbook odds, which could have anchored the cancellation-risk component.

The 82% event-run probability is asserted rather than derived; a small sub-forecast or reference-class analysis of how often confirmed, ticketed golf events get cancelled ~4 weeks out would have added rigor (the agent's own process_reflection acknowledges this).

Relied on WebFetch attempts that repeatedly failed (Wikipedia 403s, Sportico CSS-only, sibling-tool errors), leaving some detail (WARN Act specifics, full schedule) gathered only via snippets rather than full-text confirmation.

issues: mcp__asknews__do_news_research rejected non-deepseek models for Metaculus users (403000).
mcp__asknews__search_news failed on n_articles>10 tier restriction (403000) and then hit a credit overage limit (403015).
Multiple WebFetch calls returned 403 (Wikipedia) or CSS-only content (Sportico); a few sibling tool calls errored.
These forced fallbacks but did not degrade the final forecast since web_search covered the same evidence.
